message:
feat(fsqlite-vfs): initialize WAL SHM header for legacy SQLite readers

Build and write a valid WalIndexHdr + WalCkptInfo into the *-shm file
when acquiring WAL_WRITE_LOCK so that legacy SQLite processes can start
read transactions without spinning in walTryBeginRead() and returning
SQLITE_PROTOCOL.

New constants: SQLITE_WALINDEX_PGSZ (32 KiB), WAL_SHM_HEADER_BYTES
(136), WAL_INDEX_VERSION (3007000), WAL_READMARK_NOT_USED (0xFFFFFFFF).

New helpers:
- sqlite_wal_path() derives the -wal path from a db path
- sqlite_page_size_from_db_header() reads the 2-byte page size from
  the SQLite file header (offset 16-17), handling the 1 = 65536 encoding
- sqlite_wal_checksum_native_8byte_chunks() implements the exact WAL
  checksum algorithm from wal.c using native-endian wrapping add
- build_empty_sqlite_wal_shm_header() constructs the full 136-byte SHM
  header: two identical WalIndexHdr copies + WalCkptInfo with unused
  read marks
- sqlite_wal_shm_header_is_valid() validates existing headers

compat_writer_init_wal_shm_header_if_needed() called after acquiring
the write lock: ensures the shm file is >= 32 KiB, validates existing
headers (skips if already valid), refuses to initialize when the WAL
has content, reads page size + count from the db header, builds and
writes the header, then verifies via read-back.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
